def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    window = params['window']
    atr_multiplier = params['atr_multiplier']
    atr_window = params['atr_window']
    
    # Calculate support/resistance levels
    df['high_rolling'] = df['high'].rolling(window=window).max()
    df['low_rolling'] = df['low'].rolling(window=window).min()
    
    # Calculate ATR for volatility filter
    df['atr'] = ta.volatility.AverageTrueRange(
        high=df['high'], 
        low=df['low'], 
        close=df['close'], 
        window=atr_window
    ).average_true_range()
    
    # Generate signals
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    
    # Long signal: close breaks above resistance with sufficient volatility
    long_condition = (df['close'] > df['high_rolling'].shift(1)) & (df['atr'] > df['atr'].mean() * atr_multiplier)
    signals.loc[long_condition] = 1
    
    # Short signal: close breaks below support with sufficient volatility
    short_condition = (df['close'] < df['low_rolling'].shift(1)) & (df['atr'] > df['atr'].mean() * atr_multiplier)
    signals.loc[short_condition] = -1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: EUR/GBP exhibits breakout behavior when price breaches recent support/resistance levels with confirmation from increased volatility measured by ATR.
